An update from Spheria Emerging Cos. Ltd. ( (AU:SEC) ) is now available.

Spheria Emerging Companies Limited reported an estimated pre-tax net tangible asset backing of $2.305 per share as at 23 April 2026. This NTA figure incorporates the impact of a 1.1 cent per share dividend, which went ex-dividend on 23 April and is payable on 30 April 2026.

The company emphasised that the disclosed NTA is unaudited and approximate, reflecting daily portfolio valuations, cash balances and adjustments for management and performance fees, as well as operating costs and relevant tax items. The update provides investors with a current valuation benchmark for the investment company’s shares and portfolio performance.

More about Spheria Emerging Cos. Ltd.

Spheria Emerging Companies Limited is an Australian listed investment company trading on the ASX under the ticker SEC. It focuses on a portfolio of emerging companies, with its net tangible assets derived from the daily value of its investment holdings and cash, adjusted for fees, operating costs and tax positions relevant to its structure.

data.title: Kitron to Double Swedish Plant as Defence Demand Drives Major Expansion
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Kitron ASA ( (GB:0F0J) ) is now available.

Kitron ASA plans a major expansion of its Swedish subsidiary Kitron Eltech in Örnsköldsvik, driven largely by anticipated growth in defence programmes that will require more production capacity over the coming years. The move follows the recent acquisition of DeltaNordic and reflects Kitron’s view of the Örnsköldsvik organisation as a strategic hub within its broader growth ambitions.

The company will build and own a new facility with 7,300 square metres of production space plus offices, roughly doubling the size of the current leased site and enabling a substantial ramp-up in output. Kitron projects headcount at the location to rise from about 120 to around 300 employees as it scales to meet its order book, including work for key customer BAE Systems Hägglunds, signalling a deeper commitment to the local community and to defence-sector clients.

Kitron has signed a letter of intent with the municipality of Örnsköldsvik to acquire a plot about five kilometres south of the city centre for the new plant, with construction scheduled to start in early 2027 and completion targeted for early 2028. The investment underscores the company’s strategy to strengthen its position in the defence and advanced electronics markets by securing owned, modern capacity in a critical Nordic location.

More about Kitron ASA

Kitron ASA is a leading Scandinavian electronics manufacturing services provider serving the connectivity, electrification, industrial, medical devices and defence/aerospace sectors. The group operates across Europe, Asia and the United States, employs about 3,300 people and generated revenues of EUR 738 million in 2025, underscoring its scale and diversified global footprint.

Its subsidiary Kitron Eltech, formerly DeltaNordic, is part of this network and contributes to Kitron’s capabilities in high-reliability electronics, particularly for demanding defence and industrial customers. The Swedish operations in Örnsköldsvik play a central role in supporting key clients and handling complex production programmes.

Average Trading Volume: 825,304

Current Market Cap: NOK21.06B

Find detailed analytics on 0F0J stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Gulf Marine Services shifts registered office to new London address
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Gulf Marine Services ( (GB:GMS) ) has provided an announcement.

Gulf Marine Services PLC has changed its registered office address in the UK to Ground Floor, Heritage House, 2-14 Shortlands, London, W6 8DJ, effective 24 April 2026. The move updates the formal corporate domicile location for the London-listed offshore marine services provider and may reflect an administrative or strategic realignment of its London presence.

The company continues to position itself as a key player in offshore energy support, leveraging a relatively young and versatile SESV fleet that can operate across multiple geographies and water depths. Its focus on both opex-led maintenance and capex-led installation work in oil, gas and offshore wind underpins its role in supporting global energy infrastructure and the growing offshore renewables segment.

Spark’s Take on GMS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GMS is a Neutral.

The score is primarily supported by improving financial strength (deleveraging, sustained profitability, and generally positive free cash flow), but is held back by the 2025 net-income decline and a sharp drop in 2025 free cash flow. Technical indicators are mixed and valuation appears mid-range, providing limited additional upside to the overall score.

To see Spark’s full report on GMS stock, click here.

More about Gulf Marine Services

Gulf Marine Services PLC, listed on the London Stock Exchange and founded in Abu Dhabi in 1977, is a global provider of advanced self-propelled self-elevating support vessels serving the offshore energy sector. Operating a young fleet of 15 SESVs from bases in the UAE, Saudi Arabia and Qatar, it supports offshore oil, gas and wind clients across the Middle East, South East Asia, West Africa, North America, the Gulf of Mexico and Europe.

The company’s K-Class, S-Class and E-Class vessels work in water depths of 45m to 80m and support a wide range of opex- and capex-led activities including offshore platform maintenance, well intervention, and wind turbine installation and servicing. Their four-legged, self-propelled design enhances cost and time efficiency by eliminating the need for tugs and offering large, flexible deck space, crane capacity and accommodation for up to 300 personnel.

Average Trading Volume: 5,129,880

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: £206.5M

Noritsu Koki Co., Ltd. ( (JP:7744) ) has issued an update.

Noritsu Koki Co., Ltd. has completed the disposition of 30,768 treasury shares as restricted share-based compensation, following a board resolution passed on March 26, 2026. The shares, priced at ¥2,145 each for a total of about ¥65.99 million, were allocated mainly to two directors and two executive officers, excluding audit and outside directors.

The transaction reinforces the company’s use of equity-based incentives to align management’s interests with those of shareholders and to strengthen its governance framework. By distributing treasury shares to core executives, Noritsu Koki signals a continued focus on performance-linked compensation, which may support long-term value creation and enhance accountability to investors.

data.title: Asia Pile to Make Vietnamese Concrete Maker TDLA a Consolidated Subsidiary
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

ASIA PILE HOLDINGS Corporation ( (JP:5288) ) has provided an update.

Asia Pile Holdings’ Vietnamese unit Phan Vu Investment Corporation will raise its stake in Thu Duc Long An Centrifugal Concrete Joint Stock Company from 45.9% to 52.2%, converting the Vietnamese concrete products maker from an equity-method affiliate into a consolidated subsidiary. The move aligns with the group’s medium-term “New 5Year Plan” to build a foundation construction business in ASEAN comparable in quality to Japan and boosts production capacity and market share in Vietnam amid strong public and private construction demand, though the financial impact on Asia Pile’s results for the year ending March 2027 is still under review.

More about ASIA PILE HOLDINGS Corporation

Asia Pile Holdings Corporation is a Japanese group specializing in foundation construction, with a focus on manufacturing, sales and installation of concrete piles and related products. Through its majority-owned Vietnamese subsidiary Phan Vu Investment Corporation, the company is expanding its presence in the ASEAN region, targeting infrastructure and construction demand in high-growth markets such as Vietnam.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen52.18B

data.title: Takeovers Panel Reshapes Orders in Humm Group Dispute, Limits TAG Voting Power
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Humm Group Limited ( (AU:HUM) ).

Australia’s Takeovers Panel review body has overturned earlier orders relating to Humm Group Limited, replacing a requirement for The Abercrombie Group to sell shares with a restriction on voting rights over 15 million Humm shares for about six months, including at the upcoming extraordinary general meeting. The Panel has also ordered enhanced disclosure by Humm’s independent board committee on the likelihood of the Credit Corp proposal progressing, required TAG to retain and, under certain conditions, tender at least 15 million shares into any successful Credit Corp bid, restricted TAG’s use of the creep exception, and directed Humm to further adjourn the EGM so shareholders can properly assess the new information.

These measures aim to restore an efficient, competitive and informed market after TAG’s December 2025 share acquisitions were found to have undermined market integrity, while giving Humm shareholders more time and information to evaluate Credit Corp’s potential takeover bid. The outcome rebalances influence over Humm’s strategic direction, limits TAG’s immediate voting power without forcing divestment, and could materially affect the dynamics and perceived fairness of any forthcoming Credit Corp offer and related shareholder decision-making.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:HUM) stock is a Buy with a A$0.87 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Humm Group Limited stock, see the AU:HUM Stock Forecast page.

More about Humm Group Limited

Humm Group Limited operates in the financial services sector, focusing on consumer and commercial finance solutions. The company’s activities include facilitating credit and related products, and it is currently the subject of corporate interest from Credit Corp, highlighting its role and positioning in the Australian credit market.

Average Trading Volume: 754,098

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$315.5M

data.title: Intertek Moves to Appoint Deloitte as New Auditor After Tender Process
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Intertek ( (GB:ITRK) ) is now available.

Intertek Group plc has confirmed a planned change of auditor, proposing Deloitte LLP as its independent auditor for the financial year ending 31 December 2026, subject to shareholder approval at the Annual General Meeting on 20 May 2026. The move follows a formal tender process and is part of the company’s ongoing governance and oversight of its financial reporting.

PriceWaterhouseCoopers LLP has resigned as auditor with effect from 23 April 2026 and has stated there are no reasons for ceasing to hold office or matters requiring the attention of shareholders or creditors. The orderly transition, with PwC’s neutral resignation statement and Intertek’s public thanks, signals a routine rotation that maintains confidence in the company’s audit integrity and regulatory compliance.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:ITRK) stock is a Buy with a £56.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Intertek stock, see the GB:ITRK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ITRK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ITRK is a Neutral.

The score reflects strong underlying business quality (steady growth, solid margins, and positive free cash flow) and supportive valuation (low P/E and ~4% yield). These positives are tempered by clear technical weakness (trading well below key moving averages with negative MACD) and rising balance-sheet/cash-flow risk signals in 2025, despite constructive 2026 guidance.

To see Spark’s full report on ITRK stock, click here.

More about Intertek

Intertek Group plc is a global provider of Total Quality Assurance services, supporting industries worldwide through a network of more than 1,000 laboratories and offices across over 100 countries. The company offers Assurance, Testing, Inspection and Certification solutions focused on enhancing the safety, quality and reliability of customers’ operations and supply chains.

Average Trading Volume: 726,805

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: £7.44B
